# Data Integrity Checklist: SPEC-001 Foundation Migrations

**Purpose**: Validate whether the SPEC-001 requirements and plan define additive, idempotent, schema-truthful SQLite migration behavior clearly enough for implementation and review.
**Created**: 2026-04-25
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` workflow based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are additive, idempotent expectations defined for all M53-M61 changes rather than only selected migrations? [Completeness, Spec section FR-001, Spec section FR-002]
- [x] CHK002 Do the requirements explicitly cover both schema creation and data mutation rerun safety, including columns, tables, indexes, backfills, and seeds? [Completeness, Spec section FR-002, Spec section SC-002]
- [x] CHK003 Are the production-shape upgrade targets and expected post-migration persistence surfaces fully enumerated so reviewers can tell what data integrity must hold after one forward pass? [Completeness, Spec section User Story 1, Spec section SC-001, Spec section SC-004]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK004 Is the required duplicate-column protection specific enough to prevent unguarded `ALTER TABLE ... ADD COLUMN` reruns on SQLite? [Clarity, Spec section Clarifications, Spec section FR-002]
- [x] CHK005 Are the live schema-truth names stated unambiguously for the table and columns that must not drift: `workflow_templates`, `workspaces.name`, `agents.workspace_path`, and application-level `tasks.status` validation? [Clarity, Spec section FR-004, Spec section FR-005, Spec section FR-007, Spec section FR-009]
- [x] CHK006 Is the `facility` seed tenant-resolution rule precise enough to rule out `tenant_id=1` hardcoding and ambiguous tenant selection on rerun? [Clarity, Spec section Clarifications, Spec section FR-012]
- [x] CHK007 Is the partial unique index requirement explicit about both key order and the `slug IS NOT NULL` predicate so implementation cannot over-constrain null rows? [Clarity, Spec section Clarifications, Spec section FR-005]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK008 Do the spec and plan stay consistent about schema authority, with `src/lib/migrations.ts` owning M53-M61 while `src/lib/schema.sql` remains reference-only for SPEC-001? [Consistency, Plan section Summary, Plan section Technical Context, Plan section Phase 0 Research Decisions]
- [x] CHK009 Do the no-drift safety constraints align across the spec and plan for `agents.workspace_path`, `workflow_templates`, `workspaces.name`, and the no-DB-CHECK `tasks.status` contract? [Consistency, Spec section FR-004, Spec section FR-005, Spec section FR-007, Spec section FR-014, Plan section Constitution Check]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK010 Are success criteria measurable enough to detect both first-run safety and immediate rerun safety without relying on implied reviewer judgment? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec section SC-001, Spec section SC-002, Spec section SC-003, Spec section SC-004]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK011 Are partial-rollout and duplicate rerun scenarios explicitly covered for columns, indexes, and seeds rather than only the happy path migration pass? [Coverage, Spec section Edge Cases, Spec section FR-002, Spec section FR-012]
- [x] CHK012 Is fresh-install ordering addressed clearly enough in the plan so reviewers know SPEC-001 should rely on forward migrations, not unverified `schema.sql` edits, for the Phase 0 schema tail? [Coverage, Plan section Summary, Plan section Phase 0 Research Decisions]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK013 Is the tenant-data assumption documented clearly enough that implementers know M59 depends on live tenant rows and does not invent a fallback tenant identifier? [Assumption, Spec section Assumptions, Spec section FR-012]

## Notes

- Optional pre/post checklist git-commit hooks were not executed because this run is explicitly no-commit.
